title: Mustardy Flaky Brazilian Elderflower Grill
description: Energizing Brazilian dish featuring flaky crust, tangy mustard sauce and delicate elderflower flavors. Perfect for a quick lacto-vegetarian lunch or dinner.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
Mix together elderflower syrup, mustard seeds, and sour cream to create a tangy sauce.
Roll out flaky pastry dough on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of about 1/4 inch.
Cut out squares of the dough about 6 inches per side.
Brush the edges of the squares with a little water.
Place a tablespoon of the mustard sauce in the center of each square.
Fold the pastry squares in half to form triangles, pressing the edges to seal.
Brush the tops of the pastry triangles with a little oil.
Place the pastry triangles on the grill, seam-side down.
Grill for about 2-3 minutes on each side, until the pastry is golden brown.
Transfer the grills to a wire rack to cool slightly.
Garnish with chopped chives, grated parmesan cheese, and crispy fried shallots.
Drizzle with balsamic glaze and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Serve immediately, garnished with a few sprigs of fresh elderflowers and a few leaves of parsley.
Enjoy the combination of flaky crust, tangy mustard sauce, and delicate elderflower flavors.
Be sure to adjust the seasoning as needed, adding more salt or pepper to taste.
Serve as a main dish or as a side, depending on your preference.
Consider using leftover pastry dough to make additional pastries or to use up any remaining mustard sauce.
Refrigerate or freeze leftover pastry for up to a week, and reheat as needed.
